layout.title: Open-Q
layout.pilot_mode: Pilot mode: changes are temporary & data will not be saved

step_welcome.what: Read the research objectives and the specific instructions for participation.
step_welcome.why: Beyond standard ethical consent, this ensures you grasp the specific 'condition of instruction'—the precise lens or scenario you must adopt to evaluate the statements—which is essential for data validity.

step_presort.what: Provide background information to help characterize your participant profile.
step_presort.why: This context allows the research team to analyze how distinct viewpoints (factors) may or may not correlate with specific demographic variables or professional backgrounds within the participant group.

step_rough.what: Sort the deck of statements into three initial piles based on your immediate reaction.
step_rough.why: This preparatory step is designed to reduce cognitive load; it allows you to familiarize yourself with the full range of the topic (the Q-set) before the more demanding task of comparative ranking begins.

step_fine.what: Place the statements on the grid by ranking them relative to one another, from those you most agree with (to the right) to those you most disagree with (to the left). Starting with the extremes might help you in this task.
step_fine.why: The grid's structure imposes a 'forced distribution' that prevents you from rating everything as equally important; it compels you to make trade-offs, thereby modelling the relative significance of each idea in your specific belief system.

step_post.what: Confirm your grid arrangement and add comments explaining your strongest choices.
step_post.why: Your qualitative explanations are critical for validating the statistical analysis; they help the researchers interpret the data through your logic rather than imposing their own assumptions on your choices.

consent.label: I confirm that i have read the above information and consent to the processing of my data for the purposes of this study.
consent.error: Please accept the terms to continue.

welcome.reset_confirm: Are you sure you want to start a new session? This will clear your current progress.
welcome.default_instructions:
1. **Let's meet:** First, you will be asked a few short, preliminary questions.
2. **First impressions:** You will read through the different statements and roughly sort them according to your personal viewpoint: whether you tend to agree, feel neutral, or disagree with them.
3. **Your perspective:** During the sorting stage, you will place the statements on a grid to refine your viewpoint. You will sort and rank the statements from “most agree” to “most disagree” by placing each statement in the corresponding box.
4. **Why:** Before the session ends, you will be asked a few clarifying questions to help us better understand your most significant choices.

profile.title: Let's meet
profile.description: First, you will be asked a few short, preliminary questions.

rough.title: First impressions
rough.description: You will read through the different statements and roughly sort them according to your personal viewpoint: whether you tend to agree, feel neutral, or disagree with them. It will make the next task easier.

fine.title: Your perspective
fine.description: During the sorting stage, you will place the statements on a grid to refine your viewpoint. You will sort and rank the statements from “most agree” to “most disagree” by placing each statement in the corresponding box. There are no right or wrong answers. The best answer is the one that reflects your opinion, or beliefs.

post.title: Why
post.description: Before the session ends, you will be asked a few clarifying questions to help us better understand your most significant choices, as well as a few questions about your background.

consent.title: Informed consent and data processing agreement
consent.subtitle: Please read the following information carefully.
consent.record_error: Could not save consent record. You may continue.
consent.default_text:
1. Nature of the study and participant understanding. You understand that this exercise employs q-methodology, a research technique designed to model subjective viewpoints. The task requires you to sort statements according to their significance relative to your personal perspective; consequently, there are no objective "right" or "wrong" answers.

2. Data processing and pseudonymization (GDPR compliance).

De-identification and retention:. This technical process ensures that your digital footprint cannot be used to re-identify you once the session ends.

Exception for follow-up: if you voluntarily provide your contact details at the end of the study for a follow-up contact, the link between your identity (email) and your response will be maintained strictly for the duration of that specific follow-up phase.

Reporting: the final results will be presented in an anonymized format. Data will be aggregated to reveal shared social perspectives (factors). Qualitative comments may be quoted to contextualize these factors but will be screened to remove revealing details.

3. Voluntary participation and right to withdraw. You maintain the right to discontinue the sorting process and close your browser at any time prior to submission without penalty.

Pre-submission: if you withdraw before finalizing your sort, no partial data will be retained.

Post-submission: due to the statistical nature of q-methodology (factor analysis), once your data has been submitted and aggregated into the dataset, individual withdrawal may not be feasible.

4. Data retention and rights

presort.description: Please answer a few questions to help us situate your viewpoint.

header.subtitle: Swipe **left** to disagree, **right** to agree, or **down** to be neutral.

methodology.extremes: Tip: start with what is most obvious to you (the extremes).
methodology.vertical: In the same column, the order from top to bottom doesn't matter.
methodology.constraint: You have to fit everything! It can be a puzzle, but that's how we prioritize choices.
methodology.relative: Reminder: items are sorted relative to each other (relative agreement).
methodology.zoom: Feel free to zoom in on different parts of the grid for better visibility.
methodology.flexibility: Nothing is final: you can move cards or put them back in the deck at any time.

extreme.placeholder: E.g., a specific event, a strong belief, or a particular word in the statement...
extreme.min_chars: A few words are enough to help us understand the context.

optional.title: Did any statements feel particularly surprising, unclear, or confusing to you? If so, why?
optional.description: You are also welcome to add comments to any other statement if you would like to further explain your choices.

post.validation_error: Please fill in all required fields.
post.text_audio_info: For text + audio questions, you can respond with text, an audio recording, or both.
post.text_audio_required: A few words or an audio recording will help us understand your viewpoint.

analysis.title: Analysis
analysis.description: Factor analysis of Q-sort data — extract viewpoints from participant responses
analysis.configuration_description: Select extraction method, number of factors, and rotation
analysis.scree_hint: Each bar shows how much variance a factor explains (its eigenvalue). The dashed line marks eigenvalue = 1 (Kaiser criterion). Click a bar to select the number of factors to extract.
analysis.too_few_participants: Need at least 2 completed participants to run analysis.
analysis.eigenvalue_error: Failed to load analysis data. Please try again.
analysis.scree_plot_label: Scree plot showing eigenvalues for {{n}} factors
analysis.empty_state: Configure parameters above and click "Run Analysis" to extract factors from your Q-sort data.
analysis.caption_statements: Statement z-scores and factor arrays per factor
analysis.manual_flag_hint: Click a loading cell to flag/unflag a participant for that factor. Re-run analysis to update results.
analysis.no_flagged_participants: No participants flagged for any factor. Try adjusting the number of factors or flagging method.
analysis.no_statement_scores: No statement scores available. Ensure participants are flagged for at least one factor.
analysis.loadings_help: Factor loadings show how strongly each participant's sort correlates with each factor. Highlighted cells exceed the significance threshold shown above.
analysis.factor_array_help: Composite Q-sort showing the idealized sort pattern for this factor. Statements are placed in the distribution based on their weighted z-scores.
analysis.statements_help: Z-scores show each factor's standardized position on each statement. D = distinguishing (significantly different between factors), C = consensus (no significant differences).
analysis.z_scores_description: Z-scores and factor array positions per statement
analysis.characteristics_help: Eigenvalues reflect the amount of variance explained. Composite reliability reflects internal consistency among flagged sorts. SE of factor scores reflects the precision of the composite estimate.
analysis.help_extraction: PCA maximizes explained variance across factors. Centroid produces less mathematically constrained factors, which some Q researchers prefer for theoretical reasons.
analysis.help_n_factors: Each factor represents a distinct viewpoint. More factors capture more nuance but may split coherent views; fewer factors give broader groupings.
analysis.help_rotation: Varimax maximizes the separation between factors, producing simpler structure. No rotation preserves the original mathematical solution.
analysis.help_flagging: Auto flags participants whose loading exceeds the significance threshold on exactly one factor. Manual lets you override flagging based on your own judgment.
analysis.guide_loadings_1: Each loading is a correlation (-1 to +1) between a participant and a factor (shared viewpoint).
analysis.guide_loadings_2: Highlighted values exceed the significance threshold shown above the table.
analysis.guide_loadings_3: A "flagged" participant defines that factor. Look for clean structure: each person on one factor.
analysis.guide_loadings_4: Participants not flagged on any factor hold a unique view not captured by the solution.
analysis.guide_arrays_1: Each factor array is the composite Q-sort representing one shared viewpoint.
analysis.guide_arrays_2: Read left-to-right as most disagreed to most agreed. Extreme positions carry the strongest signal for interpretation.
analysis.guide_arrays_3: Amber-highlighted statements are distinguishing — placed significantly differently compared to other factors.
analysis.guide_arrays_4: Compare arrays across factors to see where viewpoints diverge and converge.
analysis.guide_statements_1: Z-scores show how strongly each factor agrees or disagrees with each statement (0 = neutral).
analysis.guide_statements_2: D = distinguishing (placed significantly differently across factors). Stars indicate significance level.
analysis.guide_statements_3: C = consensus (all factors agree on placement). These don't help differentiate viewpoints.
analysis.guide_statements_4: D statements reveal what makes each viewpoint unique. C statements show where viewpoints converge.
analysis.guide_summary_1: Eigenvalues measure how much variance a factor explains. The Kaiser criterion (eigenvalue > 1) is one common heuristic for deciding how many factors to retain.
analysis.guide_summary_2: Composite reliability reflects how consistently the flagged sorts define each factor. Higher values mean the factor estimate is based on more agreement among its defining sorts.
analysis.guide_summary_3: Factor correlations show how much overlap exists between viewpoints. Higher correlations mean the factors share more common ground; lower correlations mean they are more distinct.
analysis.guide_summary_4: Total variance explained indicates how much of the overall variation in sorting patterns is captured by your solution.

guidance.public: Best for high-volume recruitment. A single URL for all participants.
guidance.individual: Maximum security. Generates unique links that expire after one successful completion.
guidance.limited: Controlled access. One link that stops working after a fixed number of submissions.

grid.title: Q-Sort distribution grid
grid.desc: The grid shape forces participants to discriminate between statements, approximating a normal distribution.
grid.symmetry_lock_desc: Automatically apply changes to opposite columns to maintain a balanced distribution.
grid.auto_balance_desc: Reshape grid to a balanced semi-normal distribution
grid.reshaped: Grid reshaped to a balanced distribution
grid.no_statements: Add statements first to auto-shape the grid
grid.tooltip_desc: Q-methodology uses a quasi-normal distribution (kurtosis > 0) to ensure participants make meaningful distinctions. The pyramid shape prevents central tendency bias.
grid.locked_desc: Structural changes (statement codes, grid) are disabled because the study has collected data or is no longer in draft mode. You can still edit text translations.
grid.locked_active_desc: The study is currently collecting data. To modify the design, you must first revert it to draft mode.
grid.locked_paused_desc: The study is currently paused. Design remains locked to protect existing data.
grid.locked_closed_desc: This study is completed. The design is archived and cannot be changed.
grid.mismatch_desc: You have {{statements}} statements but the grid only has slots for {{slots}} items. Please adjust the columns below to match.
grid.unlock_hint: Go to data explorer to purge sessions and unlock structure

logo.label: Logo URL
logo.hint: Recommended size: 200x50px. Supports PNG, SVG, JPG.
logo.help_desc: The logo is displayed at the top of every study page (navigation bar) and on the welcome page. It reinforces your project's visual identity.

partners.title: Institutional partners
partners.desc: Add logos of supporting institutions or partners.
partners.help_desc: Partner logos are displayed on the landing page as well as in the study header, next to the main logo.

upload.invalid_type: Invalid file type. Use PNG, JPG, or SVG.
upload.drag_drop: Drag & drop or click to upload

data.title: Data explorer
data.description: Monitor real-time participation, analyze data quality, and manage research sessions.

actions.clear_test_runs_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete all test submissions? This cannot be undone.
actions.clear_all_confirm: DANGER: This will permanently delete ALL participant data for this study. This cannot be undone.

confirm_discard.title: Discard participant?
confirm_discard.description: This participant will be excluded from exports and analysis. You can restore them later.
confirm_discard.reason_placeholder: Why is this participant being discarded?

confirm_restore.title: Restore participant?
confirm_restore.description: This participant will be included in exports and analysis again.

csv.title: Universal CSV
csv.desc: Raw rectangular data. Best for r, python, SPSS, or excel analysis. Includes all metadata.

json.title: KenQ JSON
json.desc: Optimized for the web-kenq analysis tool. Contains study definition and sorts.

zip.title: PQMethod bundle (ZIP)
zip.desc: Contains legacy .DAT and .STA files for DOS PQMethod analysis.

table.duplicate_ip_hint: Shares IP hash with other participants

survey.title: Questionnaire responses
survey.csv_note: Full responses available in CSV export.

search.placeholder: Search by ID or recruitment token...

errors.load_failed: Cloud sync interrupted. Please check your connection.

empty.no_participants_desc: Share your study link to start collecting responses.

team.title: Study team
team.description: Manage collaborators and study-level access control.
team.invite_description: Generate a secure link to invite a new member to this study.
team.share_label: Share this link with the invitee:

permissions.owner: Can delete the study and manage team.
permissions.editor: Can modify design and view results.

participants.title: Ready to launch?
participants.desc: Share your study link to start collecting responses. Your first participant is just a click away.

team.title: You're flying solo
team.desc: Invite collaborators to help manage this study. They can view data, edit the design, or just observe.

designer.title: Tabula rasa
designer.desc: This study has no statements yet. Add your Q-set items to begin designing your sort.

settings.title: Study lifecycle
settings.description: Storage management, archiving, and study deletion.

lifecycle.title: Archiving
lifecycle.description: Archive the study to make it read-only for everyone.
lifecycle.notice: To archive a study, it must first be closed. Once archived, no further changes can be made.

danger.title: Danger zone
danger.notice: Deleting a study removes ALL data (participants, answers, config) permanently. Study must be archived before deletion.
danger.delete_confirm: Are you sure? This action is IRREVERSIBLE.

settings.save_error_desc: Failed to update settings. Slug might be taken.
settings.archive_success_desc: The study is now archived and read-only.
settings.archive_error_desc: Failed to archive study. Is it closed?
settings.delete_success_desc: The study has been permanently removed.

storage.title: Audio Storage Usage
storage.description: Monitor audio recording storage for this study.
storage.quota_help: Maximum total audio storage for this study (10–1000 MB).
storage.warning_high_usage: Storage usage is high. Consider increasing the quota or removing old recordings.

settings.title: Project settings
settings.identity_desc: Manage project identity and team access control.

general.title: General settings
general.desc: Identity and primary identification of your project.
general.slug_hint: Changing the slug will update all your research dashboard links.

settings.team_growth_desc: Need more researchers? You can invite collaborators to your project. They will be able to manage all studies within this project.

team.title: Team members
team.desc: List of users with access to this project.
team.remove_confirm: Are you sure you want to remove this member?
team.growth_desc: Need more researchers? You can invite collaborators to your project. They will be able to manage all studies within this project.

admin.label: Admin
admin.desc: Can manage project settings, members, and all studies.

researcher.label: Researcher
researcher.desc: Can create and manage all studies. Restricted from project settings.

viewer.label: Viewer
viewer.desc: Read-only access to all studies and data in the project.

invite_modal.title: Invite collaborator
invite_modal.desc: Send a secure invitation to join your research team.
invite_modal.copy_hint: Copy this link and send it manually if the email fails.

participants.title: Participants
participants.description: Manage participants and explore submitted data.

grid.select_instruction: Select a card on the grid to view details.

survey.no_answers: No answers recorded for this section.

resume.copy_failed: Unable to copy. Please select the link manually.
resume.instruction: To continue on a different device, save this link:
resume.instruction_same_browser: If you return on this browser, your progress will be saved automatically.
resume.instruction_detail: Keep this link private — it gives access to your session.
resume.restored: Welcome back! Your progress has been restored.
resume.welcome_back: Welcome back! Your progress has been restored.
resume.not_found: This link is no longer valid. If you haven't completed the study, please contact the researcher for a new link.
resume.already_completed: You've already submitted your responses. Thank you for your participation!
resume.study_closed: This study is no longer accepting responses. Please contact the researcher if you have questions.
resume.rate_limited: Too many attempts. Please wait a moment and try again.
resume.error: Something went wrong while restoring your session. Please try again.
